name: landing_page_website
prompt: Create a polished landing page for a Mac-first local AI coding harness. Include the full HTML, CSS, and JavaScript in one self-contained file. The page should have a strong hero, clear value proposition, feature sections, runtime compatibility badges, a call to action, responsive layout, and accessible semantic markup.
success_criteria: The answer should provide a complete self-contained HTML document with embedded CSS and minimal JavaScript if useful. It should be tailored to a Mac-first local AI coding harness, include responsive design, accessible structure, concrete product copy, runtime compatibility signals, and avoid placeholder-heavy or generic SaaS filler.

name: flappy_bird_game
prompt: Create a playable Flappy Bird-style browser game in one self-contained HTML file. Include canvas rendering, keyboard and click/tap controls, gravity, jump physics, moving pipes, collision detection, score tracking, restart behavior, and simple polished visuals.
success_criteria: The answer should provide a complete self-contained HTML document with working JavaScript game logic. It should include canvas setup, game loop, gravity/jump physics, pipe spawning/movement, collision detection, score display, start or restart state, keyboard and pointer controls, and avoid omitting critical implementation details.

name: eiffel_tower_image_prompt
prompt: Create a realistic image of the Eiffel Tower at night in Paris. If you cannot directly render pixels, provide a production-ready image-generation prompt and negative prompt suitable for a photorealistic image model.
success_criteria: The answer should either produce an actual image artifact if the runtime supports it or clearly state that this text harness cannot render pixels directly. If it cannot render, it should provide a detailed production-ready photorealistic prompt describing the Eiffel Tower at night in Paris, lighting, atmosphere, camera/lens details, composition, and a negative prompt that avoids distortions, text artifacts, bad anatomy/objects, and unrealistic rendering.
